title: strychnos congolana
intro: Strychnos congolana, also known as the Congo strychnine tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is known for its toxic seeds, which contain the alkaloid strychnine. The medicinal properties of Strychnos congolana include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to stimulate the nervous system. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to make tonics and remedies for a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory issues.
